title: Muslim b. Said's Expedition against the Turks
description: According to `Ali b. Muhammad-his authorities: Muslim b. Said appointed Bahram Sis as a provincial govemor.656 Late in the summer of the year 105 /723-24, Muslim undertook a military expedition, but when he did not make any conquests, he turned back. The Turks pursued him, overtaking him as the soldiers were crossing over the Balkh River. The cavalry of Tamim, led by 'Ubaydallah b. Zuhayr b. Hayyan, was in command of his rearguard and they protected the other soldiers until they had crossed over the river. (This was when) Yazid b.

sources.citation: al-Tabari, The History of al-Tabari (SUNY), vol. 24
